title: Start with Bare Chassis
description: Begin by placing both body halves on a clean, well-lit work surface. These form the structural foundation — all other modules bolt or snap into the chassis.
tips: Verify body halves mate cleanly before proceeding
Work on a lint-free surface to keep film path clean

title: Install Film Transport Module
description: The heart of the camera. Mount the brass film gate with its precision aperture, slide in the spring-steel pressure plate, install the claw mechanism with its cam follower, snap in the film channel, and press the registration pin into place.
tips: Handle the brass film gate by its edges only — fingerprints on the aperture affect image quality
Verify registration pin protrusion is exactly 0.5 mm with a depth gauge
The claw e-clip is tiny — work over a tray

title: Install Shutter Module
description: Slide the main shaft through both bearing bores, then mount the 180-degree shutter disc onto the shaft with its keyway aligned. The disc spins between the lens and film gate.
tips: Check shaft runout < 0.01 mm TIR before installing disc
Verify 0.3 mm clearance between shutter and gate with feeler gauge
Spin disc by hand — it should rotate freely with zero rubbing

title: Install Drivetrain
description: Mount the motor into its bracket, install the 2-stage Delrin gear set into the gearbox housing, and connect the gearbox output to the main shaft.
tips: Apply a small amount of Superlube grease to gear teeth before assembly
Verify gear mesh by hand rotation — no binding or skipping
Motor connector J1 routes to the PCB later

title: Install Cartridge Bay
description: Mount the cartridge receiver into the body right half. The receiver locates the Kodak cartridge precisely relative to the film gate.
tips: Align receiver registration pins with body holes before tightening
Test with a cartridge — it should click into the latch

title: Mount Electronics
description: Install the PCB bracket with standoffs, mount the main control board, and begin routing the JST wiring harness.
tips: Don't connect JST connectors yet — wait until all modules are in place
Route wires along body channels to avoid pinch points

title: Install Power System
description: Install the bottom plate with its 1/4-20 tripod insert, and snap the battery door into place.
tips: Verify tripod insert thread engagement before final tightening
Battery door hinge ears should flex — don't force them

title: Install Optics
description: Press the C-mount lens ring into the front face bore and mount the Galilean viewfinder on top.
tips: Align lens mount locating pin at 12 o'clock position
Verify 17.526 mm flange-to-film distance with depth gauge — this is critical for focus

title: Close Body & Install Accessories
description: Install the top plate, cartridge door with hinge pin, and trigger mechanism. Connect all JST connectors to the PCB.
tips: Connect J1-J7 in order — each has a unique pin count so they can't be swapped
Check cartridge door light-trap foam is intact
Verify trigger clicks the microswitch cleanly

title: Attach Lens & Final Check
description: Thread any C-mount lens onto the mount. Insert batteries, load a test cartridge, and verify operation at both 18fps and 24fps.
tips: Listen for smooth, consistent motor operation at both speeds
Run for 30 seconds to verify speed stability
Check viewfinder bright-line frame alignment matches taking lens field of view
